headline: Hasbro Headquarters Shift And FIFA Deal Reframe Investor Story
summary: Hasbro plans to relocate its headquarters from Pawtucket, Rhode Island, to Boston, with its long held Pawtucket property slated for sale. The company has also announced a new collaboration with FIFA to launch themed tabletop games ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026. These moves point to meaningful changes in Hasbro's physical footprint and its approach to global entertainment partnerships. For investors following Hasbro, NasdaqGS:HAS is trading at $97.63, with the share price up 17.7% year to...

headline: Target is making big changes to win back customers. Here's what shoppers can expect to see
summary: Target's merchandising leaders previewed the company's ambitious plans to overhaul key categories, including home and apparel, at an investor meeting.

raw.headline: Target is making big changes to win back customers. Here's what shoppers can expect to see

headline: Hasbro (HAS): Buy, Sell, or Hold Post Q4 Earnings?
summary: Hasbro’s 21.4% return over the past six months has outpaced the S&P 500 by 15.7%, and its stock price has climbed to $97.61 per share. This was partly thanks to its solid quarterly results, and the performance may have investors wondering how to approach the situation.

headline: Has Hasbro (HAS) Run Too Far After Strong Multi Year Share Price Gains
summary: If you are wondering whether Hasbro's share price still offers value after a strong run, or if you might be arriving late to the story, this article walks through what the current valuation is really telling you. Hasbro's stock closed at US$97.63, with returns of 9.3% over the last month, 17.7% year to date and 63.6% over the past year. The last week shows a 2.2% decline that may signal a pause after a bigger move, or a shift in how the market views risk. Recent headlines have focused on...
